strengths: 11+ years full-stack development with extensive TypeScript/Node.js expertise across multiple senior roles; currently at re:cinq working directly on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP development
Proven staff-level engineering judgment demonstrated through architecture work, infrastructure setup, TDD advocacy, and open-source projects (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts)
Strong problem-solving and communication skills evidenced by public talks, workshops (Web Internals, Selenium courses, TDD training), and multi-platform development (web, mobile, desktop, embedded)
concerns: Primary frontend framework experience is Ember.js, not React—no demonstrated expertise in the job's primary frontend stack despite 11+ years of full-stack work
Resume lacks explicit mention of modern testing frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Playwright) and security/authentication patterns despite these being required skills
No clear evidence of relational database expertise (PostgreSQL, MySQL required) or recent hands-on cloud platform work beyond AWS CDK; Kubernetes deployment mentioned only for open-source project
response.justification: The candidate brings deep, multi-year expertise in TypeScript and Node.js across senior roles and is currently working on required AI-agent infrastructure—a skill gap many candidates have. However, the absence of React experience is a notable misalignment with the primary frontend stack, and the resume provides limited concrete evidence of testing framework expertise, relational database work, and security practices that the role emphasizes. Cultural and architectural fit appear strong, but technical stack coverage has clear gaps.
